problem.title: Presentations are still broken
problem.pain: Every presentation tool hands you a blank canvas and says "design whatever you want." That's the problem. People spend hours wrestling with layouts. Brand guidelines get ignored. Data goes stale in old decks. And AI can't help—there's nothing structured for it to work with.
problem.solution: Deckyard works like a website, not a canvas. Designers create templates. Everyone else fills in content. The system enforces consistency. And because slides are structured, AI can generate, update, and translate them automatically.

dpa.question: Can I get a DPA (Data Processing Agreement)?
dpa.answer: Yes. Contact us at privacy@deckyard.eu for our standard DPA or to discuss custom requirements.

cloudAct.question: Is Deckyard subject to US CLOUD Act?
cloudAct.answer: No. Deckyard is operated from the Netherlands. We use only EU infrastructure providers (Scaleway). There are no US entities in our data processing chain.

audienceData.question: What happens to audience response data?
audienceData.answer: Audience responses (poll votes, Q&A submissions, etc.) are stored with your presentation. They're deleted when you delete the presentation. We don't analyze or profile audience behavior.

aiGdpr.question: Do AI features comply with GDPR?
aiGdpr.answer: AI features use your own API keys, meaning prompts go directly to your chosen provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or your own self-hosted model). We don't process or store AI interactions. You maintain your own relationship and DPA with your AI provider. With self-hosted models via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint, no data leaves your infrastructure at all.

exportData.question: Can I export all my data?
exportData.answer: Yes. You can export presentations as JSON, and audience data as CSV. For complete account data exports (GDPR Article 20), contact us.

deleteAccount.question: How do I delete my account?
deleteAccount.answer: Account deletion is available in settings. All your data is permanently removed within 30 days. For immediate deletion requests, contact privacy@deckyard.eu.

cta.title: Questions about privacy?
cta.lead: We're happy to discuss your specific requirements.

selfHostedIncludes.question: What's included in self-hosted?
selfHostedIncludes.answer: Everything. Self-hosted Deckyard includes all 44 slide types, all interactive features (polls, Q&A, feedback), AI generation, export to all formats, and unlimited presentations and audience members. The only difference is you manage your own infrastructure.

aiCosts.question: Do I pay for AI features?
aiCosts.answer: AI features use your own API keys from OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible service. You pay your provider directly at their rates—typically €0.02-0.05 per deck generation. Self-hosted models (via Ollama, vLLM, etc.) are free. We don't mark up API costs or charge AI credits.

enterprise.question: Is there an enterprise tier?
enterprise.answer: Not yet. For larger teams or specific compliance requirements, contact us to discuss custom arrangements. We're happy to work with you on dedicated hosting, custom SLAs, or specific security requirements.

switchPlans.question: Can I switch between plans?
switchPlans.answer: Yes. You can upgrade, downgrade, or switch to self-hosting at any time. Your data is always exportable in standard formats (JSON, PDF, PPTX).

paymentMethods.question: What payment methods do you accept?
paymentMethods.answer: We accept credit cards and SEPA direct debit for EU customers. Annual billing is available with a 15% discount.

discounts.question: Do you offer discounts?

meta.title: Deckyard vs Mentimeter — Open Source Alternative
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Mentimeter for audience interaction. Open source, self-hostable, no per-seat pricing, EU-hosted alternative.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Mentimeter
hero.lead: Love audience interaction, but want to own your tools? Deckyard gives you polls, Q&A, and live feedback—plus a full presentation editor.

pricing.title: 1. No more per-seat pricing anxiety
pricing.description: Mentimeter charges per presenter. At $11.99/month basic or $24.99/month pro, a 20-person team costs $240–$500/month. Deckyard's flat pricing (or free self-hosting) means your costs don't scale with headcount.

fullEditor.title: 2. Build entire presentations, not just interactions
fullEditor.description: Mentimeter excels at interactive slides but lacks a full presentation editor. You end up bouncing between Mentimeter and PowerPoint. Deckyard combines both: design complete decks with 44 slide types, then add live interaction where you need it.

dataSovereignty.title: 3. Real data sovereignty
dataSovereignty.description: Mentimeter is a Swedish company but operates US infrastructure. For GDPR-conscious organizations, that's a concern. Deckyard runs on French servers, or you can self-host entirely on your own infrastructure.

byoAi.title: 4. BYO AI, no markup
byoAi.description: Mentimeter's AI features come with their paid plans. Deckyard lets you connect your own API keys—OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible model including self-hosted. You pay only what you use, at provider rates.

openSource.title: 5. Open source transparency
openSource.description: Deckyard's code is public under MIT license. You can audit it, modify it, contribute to it. No black boxes, no vendor lock-in, no surprise deprecations.

whenMentimeter.title: When to stick with Mentimeter
whenMentimeter.lead: We're honest about trade-offs. Mentimeter might be better if:
whenMentimeter.turnkey: You need turnkey simplicity — Mentimeter's SaaS model means zero setup. If you have no technical resources and don't want to manage anything, that's valuable.
whenMentimeter.soloPresenter: You're a solo presenter — For individuals giving occasional talks, Mentimeter's free tier (50 participants) may be sufficient.
whenMentimeter.integrations: You need specific integrations — Mentimeter has more third-party integrations built in. Deckyard is catching up, but check if your specific tool is supported.

migration.title: Migration is simple
migration.step1: Export your content — Download your Mentimeter presentations as PDFs for reference
migration.step2: Recreate in Deckyard — Use our AI generator to quickly rebuild decks, or import content manually
migration.step3: Test with your audience — Run a pilot session to ensure everything works
migration.step4: Go live — Share your new Deckyard presentation URL with participants

meta.title: Deckyard vs Slides.com — Different tools, different philosophies
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Slides.com. Both are excellent tools built on different philosophies: Slides.com for visual freedom, Deckyard for structured speed. Open source, self-hostable, with live audience interaction.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Slides.com
hero.lead: Two excellent tools with fundamentally different approaches. Slides.com gives you a beautiful freeform canvas. Deckyard gives you structured speed. Here's how to choose.

respect.title: Credit where it's due
respect.lead: Slides.com is built by the creator of Reveal.js—the open source presentation framework that powers millions of decks, including Deckyard's own rendering engine. That's a massive contribution to the presentation world, and we have enormous respect for what they've built.
respect.paragraph: This isn't a "we're better" comparison. These are genuinely different tools for different needs. Our goal is to help you pick the right one.

philosophy.title: Different design philosophies
philosophy.intro: The biggest difference isn't a feature list—it's a philosophy about who controls design.

slidescom.title: Slides.com: Design control for everyone
slidescom.description: Slides.com puts visual control in the hands of each user. Drag elements anywhere. Resize anything. Change fonts freely. Every person creating a presentation makes their own design decisions.
slidescom.bestFor: Best for: individual power users who want hands-on control of every visual detail

deckyard.title: Deckyard: Design control for design teams
deckyard.description: Deckyard separates design from content. Your brand designers and frontend developers define pixel-perfect themes once—typography, colors, spacing, layouts. Then content creators simply pick slide types and write. They can't accidentally break the brand.
deckyard.approach: This is exactly how modern website development works. Nobody gives content editors a freeform canvas to build web pages—they get beautifully designed components that are impossible to mess up. Same principle, applied to presentations.
deckyard.speed: The result? Pixel-perfect brand consistency across every presentation, every team, every department. And content creators work an order of magnitude faster because they're writing, not designing.
deckyard.bestFor: Best for: teams and organizations that need brand consistency and fast content creation

slideTypes.title: 44 slide types with built-in intent
slideTypes.lead: Deckyard's structured approach enables something powerful: every slide type has semantic meaning. A "Quote" slide is different from a "Key Point" slide. A "Process" slide implies sequential steps. A "Comparison" slide suggests weighing options.
slideTypes.aiAdvantage: This structure is why our AI generation works so well. When an LLM understands that your content describes a process, it knows to use a Process slide. When you're highlighting a key insight, it chooses a Quote or Key Point slide. The baked-in semantics guide both humans and AI toward better presentations.
slideTypes.categories: Our 44 slide types cover: data visualizations, timelines, comparisons, quotes, team profiles, image galleries, video embeds, interactive polls, Q&A, and much more. Every type is styled for your brand automatically.

audienceInteraction.title: Built-in audience interaction
audienceInteraction.description: Slides.com creates beautiful static presentations. Deckyard includes live polls, Q&A, word clouds, Likert scales, and feedback collection—all in one tool, no integrations needed.

sovereignty.title: Open source and data sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Slides.com is US-hosted and closed-source. If data sovereignty matters to you—or you simply want to control your own infrastructure—Deckyard gives you that choice. Self-host with Docker, or use our EU-hosted cloud in France.

aiChoice.title: Choose your AI provider
aiChoice.description: Deckyard lets you bring your own API keys from OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible service. Run local models with Ollama for zero cost. You pick the model, you pay provider rates directly. No markup, no credits, no lock-in to a single AI vendor.

openSource.title: MIT licensed, forever
openSource.description: If Slides.com changes pricing, removes features, or shuts down, you lose access. Deckyard's MIT-licensed code means you can fork it, modify it, and run it yourself—forever.

euData.title: EU data residency
euData.description: Slides.com runs on US infrastructure. Deckyard's managed cloud is hosted in France on Scaleway. For EU organizations, that's a meaningful difference for GDPR compliance.

whenSlidescom.title: When Slides.com is the better choice
whenSlidescom.lead: We're not saying Deckyard is always better—it depends on how you work:
whenSlidescom.individual: You're an individual creator — If you're making presentations for yourself and enjoy hands-on design control, Slides.com's canvas approach lets you craft exactly what you envision.
whenSlidescom.revealjs: You're a Reveal.js power user — Slides.com is built directly on Reveal.js with deep integration. If you need Reveal.js's full feature set, they wrote the book.
whenSlidescom.noThemeTeam: You don't have design resources — Deckyard shines when you have designers who can create custom themes. If you're starting from scratch without design support, Slides.com's visual editor gives you more immediate flexibility.
whenSlidescom.staticOnly: You only need static presentations — If you never need audience interaction, Slides.com is simpler and more focused.

migration.title: Switching from Slides.com
migration.lead: Both tools can export to standard formats, making migration straightforward:
migration.step1: Export from Slides.com — Download your presentations as HTML or PDF
migration.step2: Import content to Deckyard — Paste content or use AI to regenerate slides from your source material
migration.step3: Add interactivity — Enhance your static slides with polls, Q&A, and audience feedback
migration.step4: Publish and share — Your new Deckyard presentations work on any device

meta.title: Deckyard vs Google Slides — Open Source Alternative
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Google Slides. Open source, self-hostable, with built-in audience interaction. EU-hosted alternative to Google Workspace.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Google Slides
hero.lead: Need audience interaction without add-ons? Want to keep your data out of Google's cloud? Deckyard gives you a full presentation editor with polls, Q&A, and EU hosting.

reasons.title: Why teams choose Deckyard over Google Slides

audienceInteraction.title: 1. Built-in audience interaction
audienceInteraction.description: Google Slides has no native polling, Q&A, or feedback features. You need third-party add-ons like Slido or Mentimeter, creating extra costs and complexity. Deckyard includes polls, Likert scales, Q&A, and feedback collection built right in.

dataSovereignty.title: 2. EU data sovereignty
dataSovereignty.description: Google Slides runs on Google Cloud, primarily in US data centers. Even with a DPA, your data is subject to US jurisdiction. Deckyard runs on French servers, or you can self-host entirely on your own EU infrastructure.

googleFree.title: 3. Google-free compliance
googleFree.description: Some organizations—schools, government agencies, privacy-focused companies—need to avoid Google services. Deckyard gives you professional presentations without a Google account or ecosystem dependency.

aiChoice.title: 4. Choose your AI provider
aiChoice.description: Google Slides ties you to Gemini for AI features. Deckyard lets you connect OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible model—including self-hosted via Ollama. You pay provider rates directly—no markup, no lock-in.

openSource.title: 5. Open source transparency
openSource.description: Google Slides is a black box. Deckyard's MIT-licensed code is fully auditable. Modify it, contribute to it, or fork it. No vendor lock-in, no surprise deprecations.

whenGoogleSlides.title: When Google Slides makes sense
whenGoogleSlides.lead: We're honest about trade-offs. Google Slides might be better if:
whenGoogleSlides.workspaceIntegration: Deep Google Workspace integration — If your org is all-in on Google (Drive, Meet, Calendar), Slides' native integration is seamless.
whenGoogleSlides.realTimeCollab: Real-time co-editing — Google's simultaneous editing is best-in-class. Deckyard has comments and sharing, but not live cursor collaboration.
whenGoogleSlides.simpleNeeds: Simple needs, zero budget — Google Slides is free with a Google account. If you don't need audience interaction or data sovereignty, it's hard to beat free.

migration.title: Migrating from Google Slides
migration.step1: Export from Google — Download your presentations as PPTX or PDF
migration.step2: Import to Deckyard — Upload PPTX files or use AI to regenerate from your content
migration.step3: Add interactivity — Enhance your slides with polls, Q&A, and feedback
migration.step4: Share — Deckyard presentations work on any device, no Google account required

meta.title: Deckyard vs PowerPoint — Open Source Alternative
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Microsoft PowerPoint. Open source, no license fees, with built-in audience interaction and EU hosting.

hero.title: Deckyard vs PowerPoint
hero.lead: Tired of per-seat licensing? Need audience interaction without extra tools? Deckyard offers professional presentations at a fraction of the cost.

reasons.title: Why teams switch from PowerPoint to Deckyard

noLicense.title: 1. No license fees
noLicense.description: PowerPoint requires Microsoft 365 at $12.50-22/user/month. For a 50-person team, that's $7,500-13,200/year just for the software. Deckyard is free to self-host, or €49/month flat for teams—regardless of size.

audienceInteraction.title: 2. Built-in audience interaction
audienceInteraction.description: PowerPoint has no polling, Q&A, or feedback features. You need separate tools like Mentimeter ($500+/month for teams) or Slido. Deckyard includes everything: polls, Likert scales, Q&A, word clouds, and lead capture.

aiChoice.title: 3. AI without Copilot pricing
aiChoice.description: Microsoft Copilot costs an extra $30/user/month. For 20 users, that's $7,200/year. Deckyard lets you use OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or self-hosted models with your own API keys—typically €0.02-0.05 per deck generated, or free with local models.

openSource.title: 4. Open source = no lock-in
openSource.description: Microsoft can change pricing, deprecate features, or require new subscriptions. Deckyard's MIT license means you can always run it yourself, fork it, or migrate your data.

webFirst.title: 5. Web-first, works anywhere
webFirst.description: Deckyard runs in any browser. No installation, no IT deployment, no version conflicts. Present from any device—your laptop, a conference room PC, or your phone.

costComparison.title: Cost comparison
costComparison.lead: See how licensing costs add up:

whenPowerPoint.title: When PowerPoint makes sense
whenPowerPoint.lead: We're honest about trade-offs. PowerPoint might be better if:
whenPowerPoint.m365Investment: You're invested in Microsoft 365 — If your organization already pays for M365, PowerPoint is included. The marginal cost is zero.
whenPowerPoint.advancedAnimations: You need advanced animations — PowerPoint's animation engine is more sophisticated. Complex motion paths, morph transitions, and video editing are stronger in PowerPoint.
whenPowerPoint.desktopWorkflow: You prefer desktop apps — If you want a native app with offline access and keyboard shortcuts you've used for years, PowerPoint's desktop experience is mature.
whenPowerPoint.enterpriseIt: Enterprise IT requirements — Some organizations mandate Microsoft tools for compliance, support, or training reasons.

meta.title: About Deckyard — Built in Rotterdam, for everyone
meta.description: Learn about Deckyard's mission, values, and the team behind the open-source presentation platform.

hero.title: Built in Rotterdam, for everyone
hero.lead: Deckyard is an open-source presentation platform built on the belief that professional tools should be accessible, transparent, and respect your data.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.lead: Make professional presentations accessible to everyone, without compromising on privacy or requiring vendor lock-in.
mission.description: We believe the tools you use to communicate ideas shouldn't require expensive subscriptions, shouldn't harvest your data, and shouldn't lock you into a single vendor. Deckyard is our answer: a full-featured presentation platform that's open source, self-hostable, and built with privacy as a default.

story.title: Our story
story.paragraph1: Deckyard started from a simple frustration: why do presentation tools either cost a fortune, lack essential features, or require you to hand over your data to US cloud providers?
story.paragraph2: We wanted a tool that combined the polish of commercial products with the freedom of open source. One that could be self-hosted for organizations that need it, but also offered a managed option for those who don't want to run their own infrastructure.
story.paragraph3: So we built it. Deckyard is the presentation tool we wanted to use—and now it's available for everyone.

philosophy.title: Our design philosophy
philosophy.lead: We separate design from content—just like modern web development.
philosophy.paragraph1: In website development, nobody gives content editors a freeform canvas. Design teams create pixel-perfect components, and content creators fill them in. The result is brand consistency across thousands of pages, created by hundreds of people who aren't designers.
philosophy.paragraph2: Deckyard applies the same principle to presentations. Your brand designers and frontend developers define themes—typography, colors, spacing, layouts for 44 slide types. Then everyone else simply picks the right slide type and writes their content. They can't accidentally break the brand.
philosophy.paragraph3: The result is pixel-perfect presentations, consistent across every team and department. And content creators work an order of magnitude faster because they're communicating, not designing.

semantic.title: Structured slides, smarter AI
semantic.description: Each slide type has semantic meaning baked in. A 'Quote' slide is different from a 'Key Point' slide. A 'Timeline' implies chronology. A 'Comparison' suggests weighing options. This structure enables our AI to make better decisions about how to present your content—and it helps you think more clearly about what you're trying to say.

values.title: Our values
values.lead: The principles that guide how we build Deckyard.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: Open source isn't just a license—it's a commitment to building in public. Our code, our roadmap, and our business model are all visible.

privacy.title: Privacy by default
privacy.description: We don't track you. We don't sell your data. We don't even have the technical capability to read your presentations on our managed cloud.

sovereignty.title: Digital sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Your data should stay where you want it. EU hosting, self-hosting, or air-gapped deployment—you choose.

sustainable.title: Sustainable business
sustainable.description: We're building a business that can support open source development long-term. Fair pricing, no VC pressure to grow at all costs.

hero.title: Accessibility statement
hero.lead: Deckyard is committed to making presentations accessible to everyone, including people with disabilities.

commitment.title: Our commitment
commitment.paragraph1: We believe that presentation tools should be usable by everyone, regardless of ability. Deckyard is designed with accessibility in mind, following Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) principles.
commitment.paragraph2: This statement describes our current accessibility status, the features we've implemented, and how you can report accessibility issues.

standards.title: Accessibility standards
standards.lead: We follow WCAG 2.1 guidelines organized around four principles.

perceivable.title: Perceivable
perceivable.description: Content is presented in ways that users can perceive. Text alternatives for images, sufficient color contrast, resizable text.

operable.title: Operable
operable.description: Interface components are operable by all users. Full keyboard navigation, no time limits, clear focus indicators.

understandable.title: Understandable
understandable.description: Content and operation are understandable. Clear language, consistent navigation, helpful error messages.

robust.title: Robust
robust.description: Content works with current and future technologies. Semantic HTML, ARIA labels, screen reader compatibility.

editor.title: Editor accessibility
editor.keyboard: Full keyboard navigation for all editor functions
editor.screenReader: Screen reader announcements for actions and changes
editor.focusIndicators: Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements
editor.colorContrast: Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards
editor.textScaling: Interface scales with browser text size settings

presentations.title: Presentation accessibility
presentations.altText: Alt text support for all images
presentations.headings: Proper heading hierarchy in exported content
presentations.colorNotAlone: Information not conveyed by color alone

audience.title: Audience view accessibility
audience.largeTargets: Large touch targets for interactive elements
audience.clearLabels: Clear labels on all form inputs

compliance.title: Compliance status
compliance.wcagAANotes: Most criteria met, some complex widgets in progress

knownIssues.title: Known issues
knownIssues.lead: We're actively working on the following accessibility improvements:
knownIssues.charts: Chart accessibility — Data visualizations need improved screen reader descriptions. Workaround: include data in presenter notes.
knownIssues.dragDrop: Drag and drop — Slide reordering requires mouse. Workaround: use keyboard shortcuts for slide management.
knownIssues.animations: Animation controls — Some animations cannot be paused. Fix in progress.
knownIssues.reporting: Found an issue not listed here? Please report it to accessibility@deckyard.eu

testing.title: How we test
testing.automated: Automated testing with axe-core on every build
testing.screenReaders: Manual testing with NVDA, VoiceOver, and JAWS

cta.title: Report an accessibility issue
cta.lead: If you encounter accessibility barriers, please let us know. We take all reports seriously and work to address issues promptly.

meta.title: Deckyard vs Slido — Open Source Alternative
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Slido for audience interaction. Open source, self-hostable, with built-in slide editor. No Cisco ownership.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Slido
hero.lead: Looking for a Slido alternative? Deckyard offers the same audience interaction features—plus a full presentation editor, open source transparency, and independence from enterprise giants.

reasons.title: Why teams leave Slido for Deckyard

ciscoAcquisition.title: 1. Independence from Cisco
ciscoAcquisition.description: Slido was acquired by Cisco in 2020. If you're concerned about enterprise consolidation, data practices of large corporations, or being locked into the Webex ecosystem, Deckyard offers independence.

fullEditor.title: 2. Complete presentation solution
fullEditor.description: Slido only does audience interaction—you still need PowerPoint, Google Slides, or Keynote. Deckyard includes a full 44-slide-type editor plus interactions, so you're not switching between tools.

pricing.title: 3. Simpler, cheaper pricing
pricing.description: Slido's pricing is event-based or enterprise. Deckyard offers flat monthly pricing or free self-hosting—no surprises, no per-event calculations.

dataSovereignty.title: 4. Real EU data sovereignty
dataSovereignty.description: Slido is now a US-owned company (Cisco). While they offer EU hosting, the company is subject to US law. Deckyard is an EU company with EU-only infrastructure—or self-host for complete control.

openSource.title: 5. Open source transparency
openSource.description: Deckyard's code is public under MIT license. Audit it, modify it, self-host it. No black boxes, no uncertainty about what happens to your data.

whenSlido.title: When Slido makes sense
whenSlido.lead: We're honest about trade-offs. Slido might be better if:
whenSlido.webex: Deep Webex integration — If you're all-in on Cisco Webex, Slido's native integration is seamless.
whenSlido.enterprise: Enterprise support requirements — Cisco can offer enterprise SLAs and support that a smaller company can't match.
whenSlido.existingWorkflow: Existing workflow — If your team already knows Slido and it works, switching has a cost.

migration.title: Migrating from Slido
migration.lead: Moving from Slido to Deckyard is straightforward:
migration.step1: Export your Slido data — Download poll results and Q&A history for your records
migration.step2: Build presentations in Deckyard — Use the editor or AI to create new decks with interactions built in
migration.step3: Test with a pilot event — Run one session to ensure everything works as expected
migration.step4: Roll out — Share the new workflow with your team

meta.title: Deckyard vs Claper — Open Source Comparison
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Claper, two open-source audience interaction tools. MIT vs GPLv3, full editor vs overlay, feature comparison.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Claper
hero.lead: Both are open-source audience interaction tools. Here's how they differ and which might be right for your needs.

chooseDeckyard.title: Choose Deckyard if you want
chooseDeckyard.lead: Deckyard is the better choice when you need more than an interaction overlay.

fullEditor.title: A complete presentation editor
fullEditor.description: Deckyard has 44 slide types for building entire presentations—not just adding interactions to existing PDFs. Design, present, and interact all in one tool.

mitLicense.title: MIT license flexibility
mitLicense.description: MIT license means you can modify Deckyard and keep your changes private. Use it in commercial products without copyleft requirements.

aiFeatures.title: AI-powered creation
aiFeatures.description: Generate entire presentations from prompts, convert documents to slides, improve content with AI assistance. Claper has no AI features.

exportOptions.title: Multiple export formats
exportOptions.description: Export to PDF, PPTX, HTML, and JSON. Claper only exports to PDF.

chooseClaper.title: Choose Claper if you want
chooseClaper.lead: Claper might be better for specific use cases:
chooseClaper.overlayOnly: Interaction overlay only — If you have existing PDFs and just want to add polls/Q&A, Claper's simpler scope may be sufficient
chooseClaper.gpl: GPLv3 preference — If copyleft licensing aligns with your values or requirements
chooseClaper.elixir: Elixir ecosystem — If your team prefers Elixir/Phoenix over Node.js for self-hosting and customization

licenseComparison.title: License comparison
licenseComparison.lead: The license difference matters for how you can use and modify the software.
